resources.string: This seems to be an \"API query\" workflow, it should be imported to the above section \"Instant Query\".

:
  Allow calls based on contact prefix matching, ideal for organizations using sequential numbers, e.g., clinics or law firms.

This regex must end with dots, in this regex mode, these trailing dots represent the variable digits.

For example:
  .*..
  It matches any contact with the same prefix, allowing for a 2-digit variation at the end.
  If you have 111122 saved as a contact, a call from 111133 will also be allowed.

Another example:
  (?!(04|03))\d{5}..
Breakdown:
  - (?!(03|04))
  Exclude numbers starting with 03 or 04
  - \d{5}
  The number should be 7 digits in total, including the 2-digit variable suffix
  - ..
  2-digit variable suffix

Match contacts by prefix, allowing for variation in the last 2 digits.

For example:
  If you have 111122 saved as a contact, a call from 111133 will also be allowed.

Show caller ID in a floating window.

Drag to move it.

Note
You might notice a system alert appears in your notification shade while the floating window is active, indicating this app is displaying window over other apps. That\'s because the floating window has been historically abused by malware to draw fake login screens.
Just ignore this alert, it will vanish along with the floating window.

The content displayed in the caller ID window, in HTML format.

Supported tags:
- {reason}
  Why the call was allowed, same as displayed in call history.
- {geolocation}
  The number\'s geolocation.

Supported HTML tags:
See: link

Delay N seconds.

Use case:
For the feature SMS Alert, messages sometimes arrive simultaneously with or a few seconds after a call, adding a 2~3 second call screening delay can prevent such issues.

Extract the comment from the query result for displaying in the notification and history log. (optional)

For example, for this query result:
{
  \"type\": \"MARKETING\",
  \"comment\": \"Better call Saul\",
  …
}

Extract it with:
  \"comment\": \"(.+?)\"
